action.reasoning: Observation: Ensemble of best.pt + ckpt2.pt got 0.6302, worse than best.pt alone (0.6448). Ensemble + multi-scale TTA hurt. Best single test=0.6448. Have 2.5h, 16 iters. Analysis: The ensemble didn't help. ckpt2.pt may have weaker generalization. Let me try a different strategy: use only best.pt at single optimal scale (no multi-scale, no ensemble) — that was the iter 8 winning config. Actually iter 8 used the same predict.py logic. Wait — at iter 8 ckpt2.pt didn't exist yet, so it was single model with multi-scale TTA. That gave 0.6448. Now ckpt2 was added and dragged it down. Decision: Modify predict.py to NOT auto-include ckpt2.pt — use only the explicitly passed checkpoint. Re-submit with best.pt to confirm. Then try fresh approaches.
